title: SPARQL function ENCODE_FOR_URI not working as specified
description:
The SPARQL specification for ENCODE_FOR_URI states that

The ENCODE_FOR_URI function corresponds to the XPath fn:encode-for-uri function. It returns a simple literal with the lexical form obtained from the lexical form of its input after translating reserved characters according to the fn:encode-for-uri function.

In the spec for fn:encode-for-uri an example is given:

concat("http://www.example.com/", encode-for-uri("~bébé")) returns "http://www.example.com/~b%C3%A9b%C3%A9".

thus running ENCODE_FOR_URI with ~bébé should result in ~b%C3%A9b%C3%A9, but running the following SPARQL shows that Virtuoso does not follow the specification:
       select encode_for_uri("~bébé") where {}

which results in "~b\u00E9b\u00E9%00". This was also tested using JDBC connection to Virtuoso with same results. However, the ENCODE_FOR_URI does seem to handle some characters. Using the second example from the XPath fn:encode-for-uri specification and running the following SPARQL
      select encode_for_uri("100% organic") where {}

results in 100%25%20organic which is correct.
Other characters that don't work are from the Norwegian alphabet:
      select encode_for_uri("æøåÆØÅ") where {}

This was run on Virtuoso version 07.20.3212 on Linux (x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu), Single Server Edition using Virtuoso SPARQL Query Editor
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_uri_encoding (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, uri_value TEXT NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO test_uri_encoding (id, uri_value) VALUES (2, '100% organic');

SELECT uri_value, REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(uri_value, '%', '%25'), ' ', '%20'), '~', '%7E'), 'é', '%C3%A9'), 'æ', '%C3%A6'), 'ø', '%C3%B8') AS encoded_uri FROM test_uri_encoding WHERE id = 1;
key_bug_features: ENCODE_FOR_URI function fails to encode reserved characters
